The system processes incoming requests through a middleware pipeline. Each middleware component performs a specific function.
Request validation ensures all required fields are present and correctly formatted before the request reaches the
business logic layer. The validation rules are defined declaratively using a schema language.
Rate limiting protects backend services from excessive traffic. When a client exceeds the configured threshold,
subsequent requests receive a 429 status code until the rate window resets. The rate limiter uses a sliding window algorithm.
Caching reduces database load by storing frequently accessed data in memory. Cache invalidation follows a write-through
pattern that ensures consistency between the cache and the underlying data store. TTL values are configured per resource type.
The monitoring stack collects metrics from all services. Dashboards visualize key performance indicators in real time.
